hypothesis: If catalytic surface compositions are generated with the Chemically Informed Autoencoder plus conditional latent diffusion framework (CompGen) pretrained on OC20 and fine-tuned on HEA, then the generated shell-wise compositions will more closely match ground-truth distributions (lower Fréchet Distance), better respect allowed-element constraints (lower Leakage), and exhibit higher point-wise similarity (higher Cosine Similarity) than chemically-agnostic baselines — and this advantage will persist under reduced HEA training data via pretraining and LoRA.
